name: Cut the grass
description: Every Monday. Keep grass at a healthy height for your lawn type and avoid scalping in Florida heat, which stresses turf and invites disease.

name: Take out trash
description: Monday and Thursday nights for collection. Low effort but high consequence if missed — overflowing bins attract pests in Florida heat.

name: Clean litter box
description: Tuesday and Friday. Scoop solids and clumps to control odor and keep Waffles' area sanitary.

name: Change litter box (full change)
description: Empty box completely, wash with mild soap (avoid harsh chemicals/ammonia), dry fully, and refill with fresh litter.

name: Pool service (weekly visit)
description: Pool guy handles skimming, chemical balance, brushing, and basic equipment checks. Confirm he's flagging any equipment issues he spots so they don't go unnoticed between visits.

name: Pool filter replacement/cleaning
description: Cartridge replacement or deep clean of the filter media — typically an add-on cost beyond the weekly service contract. Ask your pool service for a recommended interval based on your filter type.

name: Pool heater service (electric)
description: Annual inspection of the heating element, thermostat, and electrical connections on your electric heat pump or heater. Catches efficiency loss before it becomes a full failure.

name: Pool resurfacing / major equipment repair
description: Pump motor replacement, salt cell replacement, or surface resurfacing (typically every 10-15 years). These are outside the scope of weekly service and should be budgeted as separate line items when they arise.

name: Inspect screen enclosure (panels, frame, fasteners)
description: Walk the cage looking for torn screens (let in mosquitoes/lizards), rust or corrosion at fastener points on the aluminum frame, and cracked or yellowed roof panels. Most issues are cosmetic/pest-related rather than structural.

name: Re-screen damaged panels / cage repair
description: A single torn panel is an easy DIY re-screen kit (~$20-40, under an hour). Full cage re-screening or anything involving the structural frame after storm damage should go to a screen enclosure contractor.

name: HVAC air filter replacement
description: Use MERV 8-11 1-inch filters. Florida's humidity and pollen load clog filters faster than the 90-day rule of thumb works elsewhere — monthly checks prevent restricted airflow that strains the system and worsens condensation/mold risk on the air handler.

name: HVAC professional inspection & cleaning
description: Full tune-up covering coils, blower wheel, refrigerant charge, condensate drain line, electrical connections, and a visual mold check on the air handler. Given the prior mold issue on your blower wheel/housing, this is the single highest-leverage recurring service on the list — it's both preventive maintenance and ongoing verification that remediation is holding.

name: Inspect outdoor condenser & clearance
description: Clear leaves, mulch, and vegetation to maintain at least 2 feet of clearance around the unit, and gently rinse the condenser coils from the inside out with a garden hose (no pressure washer) to remove dust and grass clippings.

name: HVAC repair / component replacement
description: Capacitors, contactors, blower motors, compressors, and any refrigerant work require an EPA-certified technician — both for safety and to avoid voiding warranty coverage. Treat any unusual noise, short-cycling, or weak airflow as a signal to call your HVAC company promptly.

name: Test GFCI & AFCI outlets/breakers
description: Press test/reset on every GFCI outlet near water (kitchen, bathrooms, garage, exterior, pool equipment pad) and test any AFCI breakers at the panel for bedrooms/living areas. A non-tripping GFCI is a safety device that's silently failed.

name: Electrical panel inspection
description: Licensed electrician checks for loose connections, corrosion, double-tapped breakers, and proper labeling. At 6 years old this is mostly a baseline health check rather than expected repairs, but it's the kind of issue that's cheap to catch early and expensive (or dangerous) to discover late.

name: Ceiling fan direction switch
description: Counterclockwise for summer (creates downdraft for a cooling breeze), clockwise for winter (recirculates warm air along the ceiling). Purely a comfort/efficiency tweak.

name: Toilet flapper & faucet aerator check
description: Drop a few drops of food coloring in each toilet tank, wait 15-20 minutes without flushing, and check the bowl for color — a silent flapper leak can waste hundreds of gallons a month. While you're at it, unscrew and rinse faucet aerators clogged by Florida's hard water.

name: Water heater flush & inspect
description: Drain a few gallons from the bottom of the tank to clear sediment, which builds up faster in hard-water areas and reduces heating efficiency over time. Also test the temperature & pressure (T&P) relief valve and check the anode rod's condition every couple of years.

name: Test emergency shutoff valves
description: Locate and turn (don't fully shut, just confirm movement) the main water shutoff, gas shutoff, and water heater shutoff valve. A valve that's seized shut is something you want to discover during a calm annual check, not during an active leak or gas emergency.

name: Plumbing leak survey (slab/under-wall)
description: Only triggered by signs: unexplained water bill spikes, warm spots on the floor, sounds of running water with everything off, or musty smells. Florida's shifting sandy soil makes slab leaks more common than in other regions, so don't dismiss these signals.

name: Add salt to water softener
description: Check the brine tank level and top off with pellet or crystal salt — keep it roughly half to two-thirds full. Running dry means hard water gets through and scales your fixtures and water heater.

name: Replace sediment pre-filter & test water hardness
description: Swap the whole-home sediment pre-filter cartridge (check your housing's spec — some recommend more frequent changes) and run a hardness test strip on the softened water to confirm the softener is regenerating correctly.

name: Roof & gutter visual inspection
description: From the ground with binoculars: look for missing, lifted, or curling shingles, damaged flashing around vents and the chimney/skylights, and check gutters for debris buildup or sagging sections pulling away from the fascia.

name: Professional roof inspection
description: Schedule before hurricane season (May-June) — many Florida insurers now require periodic roof certifications to maintain coverage or avoid premium increases, and a pro can spot issues invisible from the ground.

name: Roof repair / shingle replacement
description: Any roof work should go to a licensed roofer — DIY roof repairs in Florida can void manufacturer warranties and create issues with insurance claims after storm damage.

name: Stucco & exterior caulking inspection
description: Walk the perimeter checking stucco for new cracks, discoloration, or efflorescence (white mineral deposits indicating moisture intrusion), and check caulk lines at windows, doors, and pipe penetrations for gaps or cracking. Catching a hairline stucco crack early is a $10 caulk tube; catching it late can mean water intrusion behind the wall.

name: Pressure wash exterior (walls, driveway, walkways)
description: Removes the mold, mildew, and algae growth that's nearly inevitable on Florida stucco and concrete, especially on shaded north-facing surfaces. Use a low-pressure wide-fan tip on stucco to avoid damaging the finish — or hire it out if you're not confident dialing in pressure.

name: Exterior paint inspection
description: Look for fading, peeling, or chalking (a powdery residue when you rub the surface) caused by intense Florida UV exposure. No action needed most years — this just builds a record for when a repaint becomes worthwhile.

name: Stucco crack repair / repaint
description: Hairline cracks (under 1/16 inch) are a straightforward DIY job with elastomeric caulk and touch-up paint. Cracks wider than 1/4 inch, stair-step cracking, or anything paired with interior wall issues should go to a stucco contractor — those can indicate settling.

name: Lubricate door hinges & locks
description: A few drops of silicone-based lubricant (not WD-40, which attracts dust) on hinges and lock mechanisms prevents the squeaking and stiffness that salt-air humidity accelerates.

name: Window seal & weatherstripping check
description: Check double-pane windows for fogging or condensation between the panes (a sign the seal has failed — note it for future replacement, not an emergency) and inspect weatherstripping around doors/windows for gaps that let in heat and humidity.

name: Hurricane shutter / impact window check
description: Before hurricane season, do a dry run deploying shutters or panels to confirm tracks aren't rusted/stuck, all hardware and brackets are present, and you remember the process under no time pressure. The worst time to discover a stuck panel is the day before a storm.

name: Garage door inspection (lube, sensors, seal)
description: One combined session: lubricate rollers, hinges, and the torsion spring with garage door lubricant (not grease); test the auto-reverse safety sensors by placing an object in the door's path during closing; and check the bottom rubber seal for gaps that let in water and pests during heavy rain.

name: Garage door spring/cable replacement
description: Torsion springs store enough energy to cause serious injury if mishandled. If a spring is broken or visibly stretched/damaged, or if the door is suddenly hard to lift manually, call a garage door technician rather than attempting tension adjustments yourself.

name: Attic inspection (leaks, pests, insulation)
description: One annual trip up: look for water staining on the underside of the roof deck (early leak signs), check for pest entry points or droppings, and confirm insulation hasn't shifted or compressed in a way that's reducing your attic's R-value — which directly affects HVAC load in Florida heat.

name: Dishwasher filter cleaning
description: Pull the lower filter assembly, rinse off trapped food debris under the tap. This is the bare-minimum interval to prevent odors and cloudy glasses — most manufacturers actually recommend monthly, so if you notice smells or poor cleaning, do it more often.

name: Dryer vent & lint trap cleaning
description: Disconnect the dryer, vacuum and brush out the full duct run from the back of the dryer to the exterior vent cover using a dryer vent brush kit, and clear the lint trap housing itself. Clogged dryer vents are one of the leading causes of house fires — annual is the minimum for moderate use.

name: Refrigerator coil & filter maintenance
description: Unplug, pull the fridge out, and vacuum the condenser coils (typically rear or underneath) to remove dust buildup that makes the compressor work harder. Combine with swapping the PAULTRA2 air filter and EPTWFU01 water filter on the same visit.

name: Washing machine hose inspection
description: Check rubber supply hoses for bulging, cracking, or stiffness — these are a leading cause of major water damage when they fail. Replace with braided stainless steel hoses every 5 years regardless of visible condition.

name: Appliance repair / replacement
description: Minor fixes like door gaskets, ice maker parts, or accessible filters are reasonable DIY with a parts diagram for your model. Anything involving gas lines, sealed refrigerant systems, or main electrical connections should go to an appliance tech.

name: Test & replace batteries: smoke/CO detectors
description: Press the test button on every smoke and CO detector, then replace all batteries as a set regardless of whether they're chirping — pairing this with the November daylight saving time change makes it a reliable annual habit. This is the cheapest insurance in the whole list.

name: Check fire extinguisher pressure gauges
description: Glance at the pressure gauge on each extinguisher — the needle should sit in the green zone. If it's in the red, the unit needs to be recharged or replaced. Takes seconds per extinguisher.

name: Hurricane prep & emergency kit check
description: Before hurricane season: check expiration dates on stored water and medications, replace flashlight/radio batteries, restock the first aid kit, and review your evacuation plan and important document copies. Do this alongside the shutter check since both are pre-season prep.

name: Pest control treatment
description: Professional exterior perimeter treatment plus interior service as needed. Florida's year-round pest pressure (ants, roaches, occasional rodents) makes ongoing professional treatment more cost-effective than reactive DIY spot-treatment.

name: Termite bond inspection
description: Required annual inspection to keep your termite bond/warranty active — skipping it can void coverage right when you'd need it most. Florida has some of the highest termite pressure in the country.

name: Irrigation system check
description: Run each zone and walk the yard checking for clogged, broken, or misaligned heads, then adjust the timer for the seasonal swap between Florida's wet season (roughly June-September, less irrigation needed) and dry season.

name: Irrigation backflow preventer test
description: Many Florida municipalities require certified annual testing of the backflow preventer to protect the potable water supply — check with your local water utility on whether this applies to your address and any reporting requirements.

name: Tree, shrub & root inspection near house/pavers
description: Trim branches and shrubs that are touching or overhanging the roof, stucco, or the screen enclosure, and check for surface roots near pavers or the foundation that could cause heaving over time. Routine trimming is DIY; anything near power lines or requiring climbing large trees should go to an arborist.
